vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:claude-opus-4.8
vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF: let the rider control the temperature unit independently of speed/distance via an Automatic/Fahrenheit/Celsius preference

narratives.Overview: Today the ride-screen temperature unit is coupled to the Speed & Distance (imperial/metric) selection. This scope adds a dedicated Temperature control in Settings so riders can choose Fahrenheit or Celsius independently, while keeping the convenient coupled behavior as the default via an 'Automatic' option.
narratives.Problem: Some riders want, e.g., mph for speed but °C for temperature (or vice versa). The current single Units toggle does not allow that.
narratives.Goals:
1. Add a Temperature preference with Automatic, Fahrenheit, and Celsius.
2. Default to Automatic so existing behavior (temperature follows Speed & Distance) is preserved with no migration surprise.
3. When set to Fahrenheit or Celsius, the temperature unit is fixed regardless of the Speed & Distance selection.
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: A TemperaturePreference (automatic | fahrenheit | celsius) MUST be persisted, defaulting to automatic.
FR-2: The resolved temperature unit MUST be speedUnit.temperatureUnit when automatic, else the explicitly chosen unit.
FR-3: The weather display MUST update immediately when either the Speed & Distance unit or the Temperature preference changes.
FR-4: Settings MUST present a separate Temperature picker, and the Speed & Distance picker MUST no longer imply it controls temperature.
FR-5: Existing users (no stored preference) MUST keep their current behavior (automatic).
narratives.Architecture: Add TemperaturePreference (alongside TemperatureUnit) with a resolvedUnit(following:) method. AppSettings gains a persisted temperaturePreference (default automatic) and a resolvedTemperatureUnit computed property. AppModel drives WeatherController.setUnit from the resolved unit, subscribing to both speedUnit and temperaturePreference. SettingsView's Units section gets a second picker (Temperature) and a footer that reflects the effective unit when Automatic. SpeedUnit.settingsOptionLabel reverts to speed/distance-only wording.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Unit-test TemperaturePreference.resolvedUnit across all combinations (automatic+imperial->F, automatic+metric->C, fahrenheit->F, celsius->C). Update the SpeedUnit label test to assert speed/distance wording only. Settings presentation and live updates verified by build + on-device review.

narrative.Acceptance: TemperaturePreference(automatic/fahrenheit/celsius) resolves to the correct TemperatureUnit given the current SpeedUnit, covered by unit tests.

items.title: Persist preference and resolve in AppSettings

narrative.Acceptance: AppSettings persists temperaturePreference (default automatic) and exposes resolvedTemperatureUnit.

items.title: Drive weather unit from the resolved preference

narrative.Acceptance: Changing either the Speed & Distance unit or the Temperature preference updates the displayed temperature unit immediately.

items.title: Settings UI for the Temperature picker

narrative.Acceptance: Settings shows a separate Temperature picker (Automatic/Fahrenheit/Celsius) with a footer reflecting the effective unit; the Speed & Distance picker no longer implies temperature control.
